items.title: Super Netball Round-Up: Disney on Ice spoiled the sport's finals plans, so how does it secure better venues?
items.summary: The 2025 Super Netball Grand Final set a new standard with a record crowd at one of the biggest stadiums in the country. But in 2026, the sport has been forced to retreat to smaller finals venues due to a scheduling issue with Disney on Ice.

items.title: Mongols bikie Brandt Graham flees the Darwin Local Court
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Iran says ticket allocation for World Cup withdrawn days before tournament
items.summary: Iran's football federation says the 8 per cent quota of tickets allocated to Iran for the FIFA World Cup has been revoked, meaning "there is no possibility to distribute tickets to fans".

items.title: Man jailed for running over and seriously injuring SA Police officer
items.summary: A man, who was on the run from authorities in Victoria, has been jailed for eight years after mowing down a police officer in South Australia.

items.title: Foord scores in game 150 as Matildas beat Mexico
items.summary: The Matildas earned a 3-1 victory in a frenetic contest against a game Mexican side, as Caitlin Foord scored in her milestone match.

items.title: Di Blessing praises 'hero' husband's bravery during alleged Torrington home invasion
items.summary: Stabbing victim Di Blessing praises her husband's bravery during an alleged home invasion in northern New South Wales.

items.title: The ACT government wants 26,000 new homes built in the next five years. Here's how it plans to do that
items.summary: The ACT government describes its land release and housing supply program as "ambitious" but "achievable". So what are these 26,000 homes going to look like, and where are they going to be built?

items.title: Family of nine-year-old boy killed while riding e-bike describe trauma in court as drink driver awaits sentence
items.summary: The court heard Keith Andrew Chambers was driving almost double the speed limit and was "heavily intoxicated" before the crash that killed Aiden Guimaraes.

items.title: Tasmanian premier apologises for handling of Ogilvie matter, questions remain over his knowledge
items.summary: Tasmanian Premier Jeremy Rockliff has apologised and pledged to strengthen transparency around the use of public funds for politicians' legal proceedings, following a minister's resignation.

items.title: Iron ore miner Nathan River Resources enters administration owing at least $300 million
items.summary: Iron ore mining company Nathan River Resources has gone into voluntary administration owing at least $300 million in debt, unpaid wages and royalties.

items.title: Cape York figures charged over alleged misuse of Indigenous corporation funds
items.summary: Dion Creek, Naomi Hobson and Tim Jaffer are all facing multiple charges relating to the alleged misuse of money from two Aboriginal corporations.

items.title: Acting SA electoral commissioner calls for council elections to be delayed
items.summary: South Australia's acting electoral commissioner says she wants council elections delayed until mid-2027 while it awaits a review into the drama-plagued state and Voice elections earlier in the year.

items.title: Aboriginal woman pepper-sprayed by guards during self-harm incident at Canberra's jail
items.summary: The use of pepper spray on an Aboriginal woman who was self-harming after being denied access to a cultural arts program was "inappropriate and should not happen," according to the ACT's prison watchdog.

items.title: Barbeques Galore to close more than 60 stores, with hundreds of workers facing redundancy
items.summary: It will shut its 62 company-owned stores in the coming weeks and working through "transitional arrangements" with 27 stores owned by franchises, with hundreds of staff facing redundancy.

items.title: Inspector of Custodial Services releases damning report into conditions at three WA prisons
items.summary: WA prisoners are being subjected to "cruel, inhumane or degrading treatment" because the state government has allowed prisons to become "almost fundamentally broken", according to the Inspector of Custodial Services.

items.title: Family calls for 'government to listen' following fatal hit-and-run of 14-year-old boy
items.summary: Fourteen-year-old Bryson Dimovski's body was found six hours after he was hit and killed by a driver who was drinking and distracted. His family's petition calling for tougher penalties now has 20,000 signatures.

items.title: NDIS inquiry warned changes could push families into crisis and towards child protection services
items.summary: Exhausted families could be plunged further into crisis and have greater interaction with child protection services under the proposed overhaul of the NDIS, advocates say.

items.title: Fugitive Mongols bikie Brandt Graham arrested in Palmerston after five-day manhunt
items.summary: Police have arrested fugitive Top End Mongols bikie Brandt Graham five days after the 44-year-old escaped from custody last week, sparking an "extensive manhunt".

items.title: Crew rescued after US helicopter goes down near Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Donald Trump confirms reports of a US Apache helicopter crash near the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: City of Hobart e-bike library program a success and helping to drive sales
items.summary: A free e-bike library is set to continue in Hobart after a successful first year and a reported rise in sales, as more people make the switch to e-bikes.

items.title: Medical imaging provider to investigate improper Medicare billing allegations
items.summary: One of Australia's largest medical imaging providers, Integral Diagnostics, says it will look into allegations of improper Medicare billing at a regional WA public hospital where the company provides radiology services.

items.title: Brody Mihocek has surgery on neck fracture sustained in Demons' eight-point AFL win over Magpies
items.summary: Brody Mihocek is recovering from surgery to fix a neck fracture after he was stretchered off the MCG during the AFL King's Birthday match.

items.title: Idris Elba shuts down idea of a black James Bond
items.summary: Idris Elba has quashed rumours he was ever in the running to play James Bond, saying audiences are unlikely to come along for the ride if Bond is played by a black actor.

items.title: Nauiyu community leaders say residents will return to flood ravaged homes with no services
items.summary: Three months ago the town of Nauiyu / Daly River was submerged in the region's worst ever floods. Now, Indigenous leaders worry residents are returning to a community with no services, if the NT and federal government do not step in.

items.title: Severe storm leaves homes flooded in South West WA town Harvey
items.summary: An unexpected deluge has flooded homes and public facilities in the South West WA town of Harvey. The community was pelted with 40mm of rain in one hour.

items.title: Why I waited until almost 30 to take my first trip overseas
items.summary: As I approached the age of 30, I longed to travel outside of Australia, but there was always an excuse or obstacle. A chance to share my culture with the world finally got me there.

items.title: Doctors say more packaged foods should have health star ratings
items.summary: Health bodies want star ratings on all packaged foods to help shoppers make healthier choices, but the grocery industry says the voluntary system works.

items.title: New leader, old problem for WA Liberals as Basil Zempilas flirts with One Nation
items.summary: As Basil Zempilas flirts with One Nation, will the right-wing populist party drag the WA Liberals down again, or will it become a life raft on the way to a political resurrection?

items.title: Victorian Premier Jacinta Allan vows to 'see it through' despite party discontent
items.summary: The Victorian premier said she had no plans to step aside despite some Labor MPs becoming increasingly frustrated by her performance as leader and more worrying opinion polls.

items.title: Blues drop Haumole Olakau'atu for State of Origin II, Dylan Lucas to debut against Maroons
items.summary: Blues coach Laurie Daley confirms his starting line-up ahead of next week's State of Origin II against Queensland at the MCG.

items.title: A missing Tasmanian devil has been spotted on CCTV roaming around the Gold Coast.
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Wildlife carers hone search area as hunt for missing Tasmanian devil enters second week
items.summary: A two-year-old Tasmanian devil has been on the loose since last Tuesday when she leapt out of her enclosure at a Gold Coast wildlife park.

items.title: Benjamin Netanyahu looks to be pushing Donald Trump to his limits
items.summary: The US president is in deepening trouble and he does not seem to know how to get himself out of it.

items.title: Locusts in Victoria
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Darwin youth mental health ward found to be unsafe and not fit for purpose, FOI documents reveal
items.summary: The Northern Territory's only youth mental health inpatient ward failed to meet 16 national safety standards, documents obtained under Freedom of Information laws have revealed.

items.title: The criticism Marc Fennell always gets for Stuff the British Stole
items.summary: Two years in the making, the Walkley Award-winning journalist is back with the third season of his globe-trotting historical mystery series. He's treating it like it's the last.

items.title: Will you be in North America for the FIFA World Cup? We want to hear from you
items.summary: If you're an Aussie fan making the trip, or already there, share your experience with us.

items.title: Australia posts five-wicket win over England in Women's T20 World Cup warm-up match
items.summary: Australia cruises to a five-wicket victory over England in its penultimate Women's T20 World Cup warm-up match in Cardiff.

items.title: ASX loses ground, NAB sees next RBA move as down — as it happened
items.summary: The local share market lost ground after returning from a public holiday. NAB economists think the next RBA rate move is down. Look back on the day's financial news and insights from our specialist business reporters on our live blog.

items.title: Somali football referee Omar Artan denied entry to US days out from World Cup
items.summary: Highly rated Somali referee Omar Artan is turned away from the US just days before he was expected to officiate in the FIFA World Cup.

items.title: Bed block patients deteriorate during long waits for aged care beds
items.summary: As many medically well patients wait in public hospitals for a spot in aged care, their bodies begin to deteriorate at a rate experts believe is preventable.

items.title: Coalition 'indistinguishable' from One Nation, PM says — as it happened
items.summary: Anthony Albanese says the Coalition is at risk of becoming "indistinguishable" from One Nation after the opposition leader did not rule out a preference deal.

items.title: Christian Eriksen 'doing well' after on-field collapse during Denmark game
items.summary: Soccer star Christian Eriksen says he is at home with family and "doing well" after collapsing on the field again while playing for Denmark.

items.title: Cootamundra community turns out in force for Nate's 10th birthday
items.summary: A mother's plea on social media turns into a community-wide extravaganza for her 10-year-old son.

items.title: England captain Ben Stokes under investigation over nightclub incident following first Test against New Zealand
items.summary: Ben Stokes and teammate Gus Atkinson are being investigated by the England and Wales Cricket Board following an early morning nightclub incident.

items.title: Former Queensland ALP government awarded $15m contract to politically connected company despite 'red flags'
items.summary: A cybersecurity company with financial red flags received a $15m state contract in the dying days of the Miles government.

items.title: Rugby league must rise to Kane Evans's level of courage
items.summary: Kane Evans showed remarkable bravery to reveal his sexuality to the world and now rugby league must lift to his level.

items.title: Hitchhiking wallaby returns to north Qld home after 1,000km trip to Charleville
items.summary: Roadkill is a common sight on outback roads, but a group of travellers was dedicated to making sure a joey found in north Queensland survived.

items.title: Sydney's fired-up gangland a new era of 'disorganised crime' as police target powerful violence brokers
items.summary: One of the state's top police officers has promised feuding gangs bringing terror to Sydney's streets will not exist "by the time we finish" as police stare down a new era of disorganised crime.

items.title: Long delays leave Tasmanians waiting for surgery amid worries about $700m in health cuts
items.summary: Patients are waiting years for elective surgery as delays and cancellations mount in Tasmania — can the health system handle the $700 million of cuts still to come?

items.title: Record autumn rain sparks recovery across South Australia, but not all growers benefit
items.summary: Twelve months after drought left paddocks bare, record rainfall in parts of South Australia has left some farmers smiling at significant improvements.

items.title: Fire insiders alarmed over data centre expansion in Melbourne's inner west
items.summary: Serious concerns about an influx of large-scale data centres in Melbourne's west are being raised by Fire Rescue Victoria insiders.

items.title: Couple launches youth housing organisation to combat Sunshine Coast rental crisis
items.summary: Hopeful renters say negative stereotypes about young people are making it almost impossible to secure a rental on the Sunshine Coast.

items.title: The Taiwan crisis no-one saw coming, and it has nothing to do with war
items.summary: Taipei sits at the physical centre of the global AI economy but remains shut out of the diplomatic rooms where its sovereign rights are negotiated, often by others.

items.title: The most contentious parts of the NDIS bill being scrutinised today
items.summary: Politicians are presiding over three days of public hearings into a bill paving the way for the biggest ever cuts to the NDIS.

items.title: Property and the budget ease pressure on the RBA as two banks cut fixed rates
items.summary: With two Australian banks cutting fixed home loan rates, there is a ray of hope that the cycle of interest rate hikes has peaked and borrowing costs may be lower down the track — another sign the pace of economic change has moved into overdrive.

items.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: From four-time veterans to late bolter Volpato, here's your Socceroos World Cup guide
items.summary: Mat Ryan and Mathew Leckie are at a record-equalling fourth FIFA World Cup, while Cristian Volpato headlines a host of first-timers, after his late switch from Italy. Here's your complete guide to the Socceroos' World Cup squad.

items.title: Australians less satisfied with life than during pandemic as financial pressures mount
items.summary: Australians felt a greater sense of satisfaction during the COVID-19 pandemic than they do today, according to government data, when households are grappling with the impact of inflation, rising interest rates and a reduction in real wages.

items.title: Author Josh Silver channels Yellowface in satirical novel examining privilege and queer identity
items.summary: Described as one of the books of the year, Fruit Fly asks who has the right to tell which stories in an age of cancellation for cultural appropriation.

items.title: Have you found mould in your rental? Here's what to do next
items.summary: Who is responsible for a mould issue depends on a few factors, but experts say it is worth acting early on.

items.title: ABC More or Less quiz: Kings and Queens edition
items.summary: The latest ABC More or Less quiz challenges you to rank some of the most famous (and infamous) royals in history, and sort some of the current crop.

items.title: Chinese leader Xi Jinping pledges deeper cooperation with North Korea's Kim Jong Un during rare visit
items.summary: China and North Korea should strengthen exchanges "in diplomacy, law enforcement, and the military", Chinese leader Xi Jinping has suggested during a rare two-day visit to Pyongyang.

items.title: The amazing legacy of Professor Richard Scolyer
items.summary: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

items.title: Police reveal scam script found in Cambodian centre targeting Australians
items.summary: Australian and Thai police release a script discovered in a Cambodian scam compound, revealing the high-pressure methods used to coerce Australian victims.
